prose:
Method provenance
Property: Frontier orbital energies (HOMO, LUMO) and Koopmans‐style HOMO–LUMO gap (eV)
Level of theory: DFT, tier `standard` = B3LYP/def2-TZVP (RI density-fitted) in PySCF 2.x
Geometry provenance: single-point on user-supplied geometry (file: /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz); no geometry optimization (notation: DFT//user-xyz)
Solvent / environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge & multiplicity: 0, 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: PySCF (backend invoked through chemkit `frontier-orbitals` skill)
Convergence & caveats: SCF converged in 8 cycles; no optimizer involved; no warnings reported by engine.
Live log: the calculation streamed to a `.out` file in the caller’s working directory (path given at launch in the session’s live output via chemkit; reproducible via the `cli_invocation` recorded below).

Key results (eV)
• HOMO energy: –7.0468329
• LUMO energy: –0.41574728
→ HOMO–LUMO gap: 6.6310857 eV (Koopmans approximation; not an excited-state energy)

Reproducibility
CLI invocation captured by chemkit:
/home/erobles/chem-skills/mcp_server/chemkit_engine/cli.py frontier --method dft --tier standard --charge 0 --mult 1 /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/benzene/benzene.xyz --out /home/erobles/chem-skills/.fidelity_scratch/tmpkkwsu2ty/live.json
The `.json` result (path above) contains all numerical data, convergence flags, and the complete method record.

Interpretation
At the B3LYP/def2-TZVP (RI) level on the provided benzene geometry in the gas phase, the HOMO is at –7.05 eV, the LUMO at –0.416 eV, giving a Koopmans gap of 6.63 eV. These values are typical for benzene’s π-system; no experimental comparison is offered here (ask if desired).
